talk_title: Extracellular matrix regulation of metastatic dormancy via DDR1-STAT1 signaling

targets.dependency_context: collagen III matrix, dormant tumor cells
dormant head and neck and breast cancer cells

MOA_summary: Dormant disseminated tumor cells secrete collagen III, creating a pro‑quiescent ECM that signals through the collagen receptor DDR1 to activate STAT1, maintaining G0 arrest. Proline hydroxylase P4HA2 stabilizes this collagen matrix; its loss reduces hydroxylated collagens, remodels the ECM, activates mitochondria and LDH4A1‑driven metabolism, thereby awakening dormant cells.
key_findings: Dormant tumor cells produce collagen III‑rich ECM that induces nuclear CDK2 biosensor localization, indicating G0 arrest.
Collagen III up‑regulates DDR1 expression, activating STAT1 signaling to maintain dormancy.
P4HA2 is up‑regulated in dormant cells; knockdown restores proliferation and metastasis in lung colonization assays.
Loss of P4HA2 decreases proline‑hydroxylated collagens (III, I, V) in the tumor microenvironment.
P4HA2‑deficient ECM triggers mitochondrial enlargement, increased basal and maximal respiration, and higher ATP production.
LDH4A1 is up‑regulated in awakened cells, driving NADH production; its knockdown blocks awakening despite P4HA2 loss.
open_questions: What membrane receptor links collagen III‑derived signals to mitochondrial metabolic reprogramming?
Can pharmacologic inhibition of P4HA2 be therapeutically exploited to maintain dormancy?

talk_title: SPARC-mediated collagen regulation impacts immunity, autoimmunity and tumor progression

targets.dependency_context: fibroblast and tumor cells, collagen I/IV regulation

models.name: WT BM into SPARC KO host
NeuT x SPARC KO
models.genotype_or_key_features: SPARC expression in tumor vs stroma

MOA_summary: SPARC regulates collagen I/IV deposition, shaping ECM scaffolds that control immune cell localization and activation; loss of SPARC reduces collagen barriers, enhances NETosis, alters B‑cell maturation via CD24‑P‑selectin interactions, and promotes myeloid‑derived suppressor cell expansion, thereby influencing autoimmunity, AML aggressiveness, and breast cancer metastasis.
readouts: flow cytometry for CD24 and P‑selectin
key_findings: SPARC deficiency impairs germinal center formation and B‑cell maturation due to loss of collagen scaffolding and increased CD24‑P‑selectin‑mediated apoptosis.
In SPARC‑KO mice, neutrophil NETosis is enhanced, leading to increased IL‑6 and growth factor release that drives autoimmunity and CLL‑like disease.
High SPARC expression in breast cancer (ECM3 signature) correlates with collagen‑rich, immune‑cold tumors and increased metastasis.
SPARC‑high tumors produce more GM‑CSF, IL‑6, and CXCL12, recruiting myeloid‑derived suppressor cells; COX‑2 inhibition reduces these cytokines.
Knockdown of fatty‑acid desaturase 5 reduces SPARC secretion, decreasing myeloid suppression and metastasis in a SPARC‑high clone.

combination_strategies.rationale: reduces IL‑6 and GM‑CSF production in SPARC‑high tumors

open_questions: How does SPARC‑mediated collagen architecture specifically modulate neutrophil NETosis signaling?
Can therapeutic restoration of SPARC in the stroma reverse immune suppression in established tumors?

talk_title: CHIL1‑neutrophil axis drives ECM remodeling, myoepithelial loss and breast cancer metastasis

biomarkers.name: CK14
biomarkers.finding: Loss of CK14 staining indicates myoepithelial layer dissolution in CHIL1‑overexpressing tumors

MOA_summary: STAT3‑driven CHIL1 expression in mammary tumor cells recruits neutrophils, inducing NETosis and neutrophil degranulation; neutrophil elastase degrades laminin and collagen IV, dismantling the myoepithelial basement membrane, permitting tumor invasion and metastasis, while NETs create an immune‑excluded niche.
readouts: basement membrane component staining (laminin, collagen IV)
key_findings: CHIL1 overexpression accelerates lung metastasis in Middle T mice within six weeks.
CHIL1 knockout tumors retain an intact CK14+ myoepithelial layer and fail to metastasize.
Neutrophil depletion (anti‑Ly6G) preserves myoepithelial markers and prevents basement membrane degradation.
Pharmacologic inhibition of neutrophil elastase with Alvestat maintains CK14, calponin, and p63 expression and reduces circulating tumor cells.
CHIL1‑driven neutrophil recruitment leads to extensive laminin and collagen IV degradation, driving myoepithelial loss.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Neutrophil elastase inhibition blocks ECM degradation without affecting NETosis, indicating distinct neutrophil functions.

combination_strategies.phenotype: retention of CK14 and reduced metastasis
preserved myoepithelial layer and lower circulating tumor cells

open_questions: Can CHIL1 inhibition synergize with checkpoint blockade to convert immune‑cold tumors to hot?
What is the relative contribution of NET‑associated versus granule‑released elastase in ECM remodeling?

cited_papers: Kim Marske lab neutrophil elastase knockout study
